body:0002.text: A participation method is a way for people to interact with a project on the platform. Each method represents a different type of engagement, from submitting ideas or completing surveys to voting, volunteering, or reviewing documents.

body:0003.text: Participation methods help structure how input is collected, shared, or acted on. By choosing the right method for a phase, project owners can guide how the community engages and ensure the interaction fits the project’s goals.

body:0004.text: What are the different participation methods on the platform?

body:0007.text: Use this method when you want broad, open community engagement and discussion. It’s ideal for gathering ideas, comments, and feedback in a transparent way.

body:0009.text: Public idea submissions and threaded discussions.

body:0010.text: Map view to collect geospatial input (e.g. residents pin contributions on a map).

body:0011.text: ESRI add‑on integration for advanced map setups.

body:0012.text: Read more about this participation method in our support article on ‘Ideation, proposals and input management’.

body:0015.text: Use when you want participants to rank, vote, or prioritize options — especially for decision‑making or budget allocation.

body:0017.text: Multiple voting methods available (ranking, up/down voting, etc.)

body:0018.text: Supports participatory budgeting exercises (participants allocate funds to preferred options)

body:0019.text: Quantifies community preferences to guide priorities

body:0020.text: Read more about this participation method in our support article on ‘Voting and Prioritization’.

body:0023.text: Use surveys for structured, in‑depth feedback or quick insights via short polls.

body:0025.text: In‑platform surveys with multiple question types (e.g., multiple choice, open text).

body:0026.text: Embed external surveys for advanced data collection.

body:0027.text: Quick polls that display directly on a project page.

body:0028.text: Read more about this participation method in our support article on ‘Surveys’.

body:0031.text: Use this method when you want the community to submit their own ideas or campaigns for others to support.

body:0033.text: Users upload proposals or petitions for community backing.

body:0034.text: Time and vote limits can be set to control the process.

body:0035.text: Ideal for crowdsourcing initiatives and building consensus.

body:0036.text: Read more about this participation method in our support article on ‘Ideation, proposals and input management’.

body:0039.text: When a large consultation generates thousands of contributions and finding consensus feels overwhelming — it bridges open ideation and deeper deliberation.

body:0041.text: Participants react to concise trade-off statements (agree / unsure / disagree), producing a clear map of where opinions align or diverge.

body:0042.text: Results are visualized in real time for participants and admins, increasing transparency and showing how each voice contributes to the community view.

body:0043.text:
Read more about finding common ground method here
​

body:0046.text: Use when you need to keep your community informed or share results from other participation phases.

body:0049.text: Create attractive reports to summarize findings.

body:0050.text: Reports appear on the phase description for easy visibility.

body:0051.text: Read more about this participation method in our support article on ‘Report Builder’.

body:0054.text: Use this method when you want to mobilize people for events, panels, or community efforts.

body:0056.text: Recruit volunteers or participants for specific causes or activities.

body:0057.text: Manage sign‑ups easily from the platform.

body:0058.text: Builds an active, engaged community base.

body:0059.text: Read more about this participation method in our support article on ‘Volunteering’.

body:0062.text: Use when you need collaborative review of documents like plans, proposals, or policy drafts.

body:0064.text: Konveio integration to embed and annotate PDFs

body:0065.text: Users provide comments directly on the document

body:0066.text: Ideal for detailed feedback and refinement

body:0067.text: Read more about this participation method in our support article on ‘Konveio’.
